# @fluentic/style

`@fluentic/style` is a React-focused atomic CSS-in-JS library with a fluent,
chainable TypeScript API. Styles can run at runtime, and the package also ships
compiler/bundler adapters that precompile static style chains into atomic class
metadata plus collected CSS.

Use this file as the high-signal guide for LLMs editing this repo or consuming
the package.

---

## Why Use Fluentic Style

Fluentic Style is designed for teams that want the ergonomics of CSS-in-JS
without paying a large runtime tax.

Core advantages:

- **Fluent TypeScript API**: write `style(...).hover(...).media(...)` instead of
  learning a large config DSL or scattering class strings through components.
- **Atomic CSS output**: every property/selector/at-rule combination is reusable,
  deduped, and cache-friendly.
- **Build-time path with runtime fallback**: static chains can be precompiled by
  bundler plugins, while dynamic or unsupported cases still work at runtime.
- **Small runtime surface**: runtime resolution mainly maps style metadata
  to `className`, injects missing rules, and strips the `css` DOM prop.
- **Component theming without global naming**: slots and scopes let components
  expose precise override points without inventing BEM names or global CSS
  contracts.
- **Immutable composition**: builders never mutate, so variants can safely fork
  from a shared base.
- **Type-safe CSS values**: the default style function uses React CSS types and
  accepts typed design tokens.
- **Bundler coverage**: Vite, Rollup, Rolldown, Webpack, Rspack, esbuild, Babel,
  and custom compiler integrations are available from one package.
- **No required app-wide config file**: ordinary TypeScript modules can define
  styles, slots, scopes, and tokens.

Good fit:

- React component libraries that need override-friendly styling.
- Apps that want CSS-in-JS ergonomics with atomic, deduped CSS.
- Design systems that need typed tokens plus component slot theming.
- Projects that want build-time extraction but cannot give up runtime escape
  hatches.

Less ideal:

- Non-React applications that need first-class framework integrations.
- Styles that must depend heavily on arbitrary runtime data in every render.
- Teams that prefer authoring plain `.css` files and never want a JS styling API.

---

## Install

```bash
pnpm add @fluentic/style
```

React 18+ is required. Bundler integrations are optional peer dependencies.

---

## Public Imports

```ts
import {
  DefaultPrioritySelectors,
  DefaultSelectors,
  MinimalPrioritySelectors,
  MinimalSelectors,
  configureRuntime,
  createStyleFn,
  createStyleSheet,
  createStyleToken,
  combineScope,
  bindScope,
  selector,
  selectorPriority,
  setGlobalSheet,
  style,
  type,
  combineStyle,
  type CSSProperties,
  type CssProp,
  type CssTheme,
  type RuntimeOptions,
  type StyleObject,
  type StyleToken,
} from '@fluentic/style';
```

Subpath exports:

| Import | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `@fluentic/style` | Runtime API, default `style`, selectors, sheet utilities, React hooks/types |
| `@fluentic/style/jsx/runtime` | Production JSX runtime for the `css` prop |
| `@fluentic/style/jsx/dev-runtime` | Development JSX runtime |
| `@fluentic/style/babel` | Babel plugin factory |
| `@fluentic/style/compiler` | `createCompiler()` for custom integrations |
| `@fluentic/style/plugin/vite` | Vite plugin, named export `plugin` |
| `@fluentic/style/plugin/rollup` | Rollup plugin, named export `plugin` |
| `@fluentic/style/plugin/rolldown` | Rolldown plugin, named export `plugin` |
| `@fluentic/style/plugin/webpack` | Webpack plugin, named export `plugin` |
| `@fluentic/style/plugin/rspack` | Rspack plugin, named export `plugin` |
| `@fluentic/style/plugin/esbuild` | esbuild plugin, named export `plugin` |
| `@fluentic/style/precompile` | Low-level precompiled helpers such as `cx` |
| `@fluentic/style/compiler` | Internal compiler entry used by adapters |
| `@fluentic/style/plugin/unplugin` | Internal unplugin entry used by adapters |

Important: there is no top-level `select` export. Use the chain method
`.select(...)` on a style or slot builder for custom self selectors only.

---

## JSX Setup

The custom JSX runtime enables the `css` prop on DOM/SVG elements and strips it
before it hits the DOM.

Vite example:

```ts
import { plugin as stylePlugin } from '@fluentic/style/plugin/vite';
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react';
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    stylePlugin(),
    react({ jsxImportSource: '@fluentic/style' }),
  ],
});
```

Per-file pragma:

```tsx
/** @jsxImportSource @fluentic/style */
```

Use `css` with resolved items from `combineStyle(...)`:

```tsx
import { style, combineStyle } from '@fluentic/style';

const styles = {
  root: style({ display: 'grid', gap: 12 }),
  title: style({ margin: 0, fontWeight: 700 }),
};

export function Card() {
  const css = combineStyle(styles);

  return (
    <section css={css.root}>
      <h2 css={css.title}>Title</h2>
    </section>
  );
}
```

`css` accepts a nested array of CSS items and falsy values:

```tsx
<button css={[css.root, isPrimary && css.primary, props.css]} />
```

Raw style and slot builders may also be passed directly to `css`; use
`combineStyle(...)` when you need to combine scopes, slot overrides, token
overrides, or a previously combined instance.

---

## Styles

`style(props?)` returns an immutable builder. Every chained method returns a new
builder; the original is unchanged, so fork variants freely.

```ts
import { style } from '@fluentic/style';

const base = style({
  color: 'CanvasText',
  padding: 8,
});

const primary = base.hover({ color: 'Highlight' });
const danger = base.hover({ color: 'red' });
```

Numeric React CSS values follow React semantics. A value can also be a priority
tuple:

```ts
const importantPadding = style({
  padding: [2, 12],
});
```

Later classes with the same property + selector + at-rule dedupe key replace
earlier classes in `css` arrays. This gives normal last-write-wins composition.

---

## Built-In Selectors

Default selector methods:

```ts
style({}).hover({ color: 'red' });
style({}).active({ color: 'red' });
style({}).focus({ outline: '2px solid currentColor' });
style({}).focusVisible({ outline: '2px solid currentColor' });
style({}).focusWithin({ borderColor: 'currentColor' });
style({}).visited({ color: 'purple' });
style({}).link({ color: 'blue' });
style({}).anyLink({ textDecoration: 'underline' });
style({}).target({ scrollMarginTop: 64 });
style({}).disabled({ opacity: 0.5 });
style({}).enabled({ opacity: 1 });
style({}).checked({ background: 'Highlight' });
style({}).indeterminate({ opacity: 0.8 });
style({}).invalid({ color: 'red' });
style({}).valid({ color: 'green' });
style({}).required({ borderColor: 'red' });
style({}).optional({ borderColor: 'gray' });
style({}).readOnly({ opacity: 0.7 });
style({}).readWrite({ opacity: 1 });
style({}).inRange({ color: 'green' });
style({}).outOfRange({ color: 'red' });
style({}).default({ fontWeight: 700 });
style({}).placeholderShown({ color: 'gray' });
style({}).autofill({ boxShadow: '0 0 0 999px Canvas inset' });
style({}).empty({ display: 'none' });
style({}).root({ colorScheme: 'light dark' });
style({}).firstChild({ marginTop: 0 });
style({}).lastChild({ marginBottom: 0 });
style({}).onlyChild({ margin: 0 });
style({}).firstOfType({ marginTop: 0 });
style({}).lastOfType({ marginBottom: 0 });
style({}).onlyOfType({ margin: 0 });
style({}).fullscreen({ background: 'black' });
style({}).modal({ outline: 'none' });
style({}).before({ content: '""' });
style({}).after({ content: '""' });
```

Parametric selectors:

```ts
style({}).not(':first-child', { marginTop: 8 });
style({}).is(':button', { cursor: 'pointer' });
style({}).where(':focus-visible', { outline: '2px solid currentColor' });
style({}).has('> img', { padding: 0 });
style({}).nthChild('2n', { background: 'Canvas' });
style({}).nthLastChild('1', { marginBottom: 0 });
style({}).nthOfType('2n + 1', { background: 'Canvas' });
style({}).nthLastOfType('1', { marginBottom: 0 });
style({}).lang('en', { quotes: '"\\201C" "\\201D"' });
style({}).dir('rtl', { textAlign: 'right' });
```

Special selectors:

```ts
style({}).attr('[data-active]', { fontWeight: 700 });
style({}).select('&[aria-selected="true"]', { fontWeight: 700 });
style({}).merge(otherStyle);
```

At-rules:

```ts
style({}).media('(max-width: 768px)', { fontSize: 14 });
style({}).media(1, '(max-width: 768px)', { fontSize: 14 });
style({}).container('(min-width: 480px)', { padding: 16 });
style({}).container(1, 'sidebar (min-width: 480px)', { padding: 16 });
style({}).supports('(display: grid)', { display: 'grid' });
```

`media` and `container` accept an optional numeric priority as the first
argument. `supports` does not.

For default cascade priority, `DefaultPrioritySelectors` orders:
`link`, `visited`, `hover`, `focusWithin`, `focus`, `focusVisible`, `active`,
`disabled`.

---

## Slots

Use slots for component-owned style regions that consumers can override.

```ts
const buttonStyles = {
  root: style.slot({
    border: 0,
    borderRadius: 6,
    padding: '8px 12px',
  }),
  label: style.slot({
    fontWeight: 600,
  }),
};
```

A slot is callable. Calling it creates a slot override for that exact slot:

```ts
const biggerLabel = buttonStyles.label({ fontSize: 16 });
const hoverRoot = buttonStyles.root.hover({ opacity: 0.86 });
const mobileRoot = buttonStyles.root.media('(max-width: 700px)', { padding: 8 });
```

Slots support the same selector methods as `style()`. Chained slot builders stay
callable, so this is valid:

```ts
const emphasisSlot = style.slot({ color: 'CanvasText' }).hover({ color: 'Highlight' });
const emphasisOverride = emphasisSlot({ fontWeight: 700 });
```

---

## Scopes And Themes

A scope stores slot overrides. At render time, scope overrides are applied only
to matching slots.

```ts
const buttonTheme = style.scope([
  buttonStyles.root({
    backgroundColor: 'ButtonFace',
    color: 'ButtonText',
  }),
  buttonStyles.label({
    fontWeight: 700,
  }),
]);

const buttonState = style.scope()
  .hover([
    buttonStyles.root({ opacity: 0.86 }),
  ])
  .active([
    buttonStyles.root({ transform: 'translateY(1px)' }),
  ]);
```

When a component exposes a themed slot, resolve a `CssTheme` into scope targets
for that slot:

```tsx
import { bindScope, combineStyle, type CssTheme } from '@fluentic/style';

type ButtonBaseProps = {
  theme?: CssTheme;
};

export function ButtonBase(props: ButtonBaseProps) {
  const target = buttonStyles.root;
  const css = combineStyle(
    buttonStyles,
    buttonState(target),
    ...bindScope(target, props.theme),
  );

  return (
    <button css={css.root}>
      <span css={css.label}>Save</span>
    </button>
  );
}
```

`CssTheme` accepts a scope, nested arrays of scopes, and falsy values. Use this
shape for component `theme` props:

```ts
type ButtonProps = {
  theme?: CssTheme;
  css?: CssProp;
};
```

Pass theme props explicitly to descendants that should receive the same slot
overrides.

---

## Static Combination

`combineStyle(styles, ...items)` returns a cached proxy of the same shape as `styles`.
Style and slot builders become resolved CSS items with class metadata.

```tsx
const css = combineStyle(styles, theme(styles.root));

<div css={css.root}>
  <span css={css.label} />
</div>
```

## Tokens

Tokens emit CSS custom properties. Assigning a token as a style value emits
`var(--<tokenVarPrefix><id>)` in generated CSS.

```ts
import { createStyleToken, style } from '@fluentic/style';

const colorPrimary = createStyleToken('blue');
const colorHover = colorPrimary('darkblue');

const button = style({ color: colorPrimary })
  .hover({ color: colorHover });
```

`createStyleToken(otherToken)` creates a derived token that references the
original token value. A token is also callable, which creates another token with
the same token id and a new value/ref chain.

---

## Runtime Configuration

Call `configureRuntime()` once at app startup, before creating or resolving
styles, when you need non-default behavior.

```ts
import { configureRuntime } from '@fluentic/style';

configureRuntime({
  dev: true,
  trace: true,
  selectorCheck: true,
  sourcemap: true,
  classNamePrefix: 'fs-',
  tokenVarPrefix: 'token-',
  layers: ['reset', 'components', 'utilities'],
  layerNamespace: 'style',
  sheetMaxRules: 1000,
  nonce: '<csp-nonce>',
  localClassName: true,
  debugClassName: true,
  debugPropertyLength: 4,
  debugValueLength: 8,
  debugSelectorLength: 8,
  debugParentSelectorLength: 8,
  debugAtRuleLength: 10,
});
```

Defaults: `dev`, `trace`, `selectorCheck`, `sourcemap`, `localClassName`, and
`debugClassName` are false unless enabled. When `dev: true`, trace, selector
checks, sourcemaps, local class names, and debug class names default to true.

Use `createStyleSheet()` plus `setGlobalSheet()` to replace the global sheet
used by runtime style resolution.

---

## Custom Style Functions

Use `createStyleFn` to define a custom typed style function and selector set.

```ts
import {
  DefaultSelectors,
  createStyleFn,
  selector,
  selectorPriority,
  type,
  type CSSProperties,
} from '@fluentic/style';

const selectors = selectorPriority(
  {
    ...DefaultSelectors,
    groupHover: selector('.group:hover &'),
    dataActive: selector('[data-active] &'),
    rtl: selector('[dir="rtl"] &'),
    sm: selector('@media (min-width: 640px)'),
  },
  ['link', 'visited', 'hover', 'focus', 'active', 'disabled'],
);

export const { style: appStyle } = createStyleFn({
  style: type<CSSProperties>,
  selectors,
});

appStyle({ color: 'black' }).groupHover({ color: 'red' });
```

Selector signature inference:

| Selector pattern | Method signature |
|---|---|
| `:hover` | `.hover(style)` |
| `:not($$)` | `.not(selectorOrSelectors, style)` |
| `$$` anywhere | `.method(string \| string[], style)` |
| `$` anywhere | `.method(string, style)` |
| `@media $$` | `.media(query, style)` or `.media(priority, query, style)` |
| `@container $$` | `.container(query, style)` or `.container(priority, query, style)` |
| `@supports $$` | `.supports(query, style)` |
| `...` | `.merge(styleOrStyles)` |

`selector(pattern, assert?)` and `selector(pattern, priority, assert?)` are both
valid. Built-in assert types include `local`, `arg`, `tag`, `attr`, `value`,
`media`, `supports`, and `container`.

---

## Compiler And Bundler Plugins

Bundler plugins are thin wrappers around `createCompiler()`.

```ts
import { plugin as stylePlugin } from '@fluentic/style/plugin/vite';

stylePlugin({
  classNamePrefix: 'fs-',
  debug: true,
  include: /\.[jt]sx?$/,
  exclude: /node_modules/,
});
```

Compiler options:

```ts
type CompilerOptions = {
  classNamePrefix?: string;
  debug?: boolean;
  include?: RegExp | RegExp[];
  exclude?: RegExp | RegExp[];
};
```

Default transform include is `/\.[jt]sx?$/`; files in `node_modules` are skipped.
The unplugin exposes a virtual CSS module at `virtual:fluentic-styles.css`.
Import that virtual CSS from an app entry when the bundler setup needs collected
CSS to be emitted.

Current compiler behavior is intentionally conservative:

- It recognizes imports of the default `style` API and chains starting from
  `style(...)`, `style.slot(...)`, and `style.scope(...)`.
- It statically evaluates simple style objects and supported references.
- It supports default selectors, simple pseudo selectors, and at-rule chains.
- If a style chain cannot be evaluated safely, it leaves runtime behavior to
  the runtime fallback.

Do not document unsupported compiler magic as guaranteed. Prefer runtime-safe
patterns unless you have verified compiler support in `packages/style/plugin`.

---

## Atomic Output Model

Every property + selector + at-rule combination becomes one atomic rule and one
dedupe key.

```ts
style({ color: 'blue', padding: 8 }).hover({ color: 'red' });
```

Conceptually emits:

```css
.a{color:blue}
.b{padding:8px}
.c:hover{color:red}
```

When debug class names are enabled, class names include shortened property,
value, selector, parent selector, at-rule, and hash fragments according to the
configured debug lengths.

---

## Best Practices

Authoring:

- Define `style(...)`, `style.slot(...)`, `style.scope(...)`, and tokens at module
  scope whenever possible. This gives the compiler the best chance to precompile
  and keeps runtime class generation stable.
- Build variants by forking immutable builders: `const danger = base.hover(...)`.
  Do not mutate or rebuild base styles inside render.
- Prefer style arrays for composition: `css={[css.root, active && css.active]}`.
  Do not manually concatenate Fluentic class names.
- Use `.merge(...)` when composing style builders outside JSX; use `css={[...]}`
  when composing rendered CSS items.
- Use `.select(...)` for custom self selectors. Do not import a top-level
  `select`. Use `style.slot(...)` and `style.scope(...)` for nested component
  parts.
- Keep dynamic values near component edges. Stable, static style chains are the
  fastest path and the easiest path for bundler extraction.

Component APIs:

- Use `CssProp` for a direct visual override of the component root or a documented
  part.
- Use `CssTheme` for explicit slot-level theming props.
- Use slots for component internals. A slot is the library's clean replacement for
  ad-hoc global class names like `.Button-label`.
- Expose a small number of intentional slots instead of making every DOM node a
  public override point.
- In components with slots, call `combineStyle(styles, ...targets)` where the
  styles are rendered, and pass theme props explicitly to descendants.
- Use `bindScope(targetSlot, props.theme)` when turning public theme props
  into slot-specific scope targets.

Runtime and JSX:

- Configure `jsxImportSource: '@fluentic/style'` whenever you use `css` props.
- Keep raw DOM `className` for external or framework classes; the JSX runtime
  appends Fluentic classes after existing `className`.
- Call `configureRuntime()` before styles are resolved. Runtime config is global,
  so do not toggle it from inside components.
- Enable `dev`, `selectorCheck`, and `sourcemap` in development when debugging
  selectors or class output; avoid debug class names in production unless you
  intentionally need readable classes.

Compiler:

- Add the bundler plugin before relying on build-time extraction, and keep the JSX
  runtime configured separately.
- Import `virtual:fluentic-styles.css` from the app entry when the bundler needs a
  concrete CSS module for collected rules.
- Prefer simple object literals, constants, tokens, slots, and scopes that can be
  statically evaluated.
- Treat unsupported compiler cases as runtime fallback, not as a failure. Do not
  remove runtime-safe code just to satisfy extraction.
- Use `include`/`exclude` options to keep transforms focused on source files.

Selector safety:

- Keep selector strings valid for their selector type when `selectorCheck` is on.
- Prefer built-in selector methods over custom selectors unless a design system
  needs a reusable semantic selector like `groupHover`.
- Give custom selector priorities only when cascade order matters; otherwise let
  atomic dedupe and array order handle overrides.

---

## Known Invariants

- Builders are immutable.
- Slots are identified by callsite/debug data when available, otherwise by a
  per-builder counter.
- A slot override only applies to the slot it was created from.
- Combined styles are keyed by the original `styles` object passed to `combineStyle`.
- `css` props may be nested arrays and may contain falsy values.
- Runtime CSS is inserted when `combineStyle` or the JSX `css` prop resolves styles.
- Runtime code does not import compiler or bundler adapter code from the root
  export.
